fields.description:
## Why
The new impact models change scoring significantly. Following the proven plan-review playbook (ship
advisory/permissive, dogfood, then calibrate), we roll out permissive, accumulate a field corpus, and tune
the blocking thresholds down only when data + an A/B justify it.

## What
- Ship both redesigned impact models behind PERMISSIVE thresholds (no new hard blocks at rollout).
- IMPACT-MODEL VERSIONING (avoids corpus contamination): every REVIEW_RESULT sidecar records an
  `impact_model_version` tag (e.g. plan-v2 / code-v2) identifying the formula that produced its scores, so
  the calibration replay can SEGMENT old-formula vs new-formula findings and never mix them (the same
  cohort-tagging idea used for chunk-contamination in WS9). The replay refuses to pool across versions.
- Both gates emit sidecars carrying the new impact + tier-tagged sub-answers + `norm_id`/`material_fingerprint`
  join keys + the `impact_model_version` tag (code-review via the data-capture child).
- Provide the offline replay calibration (extend `docs/experiments/calibrate_plan_review_thresholds.py` to
  filter by `impact_model_version`) and a diff-grounded A/B harness that scores the labeled/adjudicated set
  with the new model and reports separation, gating any threshold-down change on beating the current model.

## Scope
- Config flags for permissive thresholds; the `impact_model_version` sidecar field + version-segmented
  replay; the diff-grounded A/B script; a documented tuning cadence. Depends on the two impact-redesign
  children and the data-capture child.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] New impact models ship behind permissive thresholds (no new hard blocks at rollout).
- [ ] Every REVIEW_RESULT sidecar records an `impact_model_version` tag; the calibration replay filters by
      it and refuses to pool scores across versions (old vs new never mixed).
- [ ] Both gates emit sidecars carrying the new impact + tier-tagged sub-answers + join keys.
- [ ] An offline replay + diff-grounded A/B report measures separation of block-worthy vs nit on the
      adjudicated set and gates any threshold-down tuning on beating the current model.
- [ ] A documented recalibration cadence (as the corpus grows, conditioned on model) is recorded.
